name: DetoXSS.Ast

unions.name: Risk
unions.comment:
Risk classification returned by the analyzer.

`Safe` means that no known risky pattern was found. `Suspicious` means that the
input contains potentially risky patterns. `Dangerous` means that a clear XSS
indicator was detected.

unions.name: RiskNode
unions.comment:
Tree node annotated with XSS-related risk information.

`selfWarnings` describe findings directly related to the current node.
`aggregateRisk` also takes child nodes into account.
cases: { node : DetoXSS.ExprParser.HtmlNode, selfWarnings : List.List DetoXSS.Ast.Warning, aggregateRisk : DetoXSS.Ast.Risk, children : List.List DetoXSS.Ast.RiskNode }

aliases.name: Warning
aliases.comment:
Warning produced by the analyzer.

A warning contains an optional source position, a risk level, and a human
readable message explaining the finding.
aliases.type: { range : Maybe.Maybe DetoXSS.ExprParser.Position, risk : DetoXSS.Ast.Risk, message : String.String }

values.name: buildRiskTree
values.comment:
Build a risk-annotated tree from parsed HTML-like nodes.

The result can be used to visualize which parts of the input contributed to the
overall risk.

values.name: classifyBalanced
values.comment:
Classify an input string using the balanced analyzer.

This combines parsing, AST-based analysis, and additional string-based
heuristics.

values.name: classifyExpression
values.comment: Classify a list of parsed HTML-like nodes.

values.name: classifyExpressionNode
values.comment: Classify a single parsed HTML-like node.

values.name: classifyString
values.comment:
Classify an input string as `Safe`, `Suspicious`, or `Dangerous`.

This is a high-level helper for string-based classification.

values.name: fromStringToNode
values.comment:
Parse an input string into HTML-like nodes.

This function is tolerant and is intended for analysis, not for browser-exact
HTML parsing.

values.name: renderExpression
values.comment: Render parsed HTML-like nodes as a readable text tree.

values.name: renderExpressionNode
values.comment: Render a single parsed HTML-like node as readable text.

values.name: renderRiskTree
values.comment: Render a risk-annotated tree as readable text.

values.name: scanBalanced
values.comment:
Analyze an input string and return a compact list of warnings.

This function is suitable for user-facing output because it removes duplicate or
overly technical warnings.

values.name: scanBalancedVerbose
values.comment:
Return the full internal warning list for an input string.

This is useful for debugging and dataset evaluation. For user-facing output,
prefer `scanBalanced`, which returns a more compact warning list.

values.name: scanExpression
values.comment: Analyze a list of parsed HTML-like nodes.

values.name: scanExpressionNode
values.comment: Analyze a single parsed HTML-like node.

name: DetoXSS.Attributes

values.name: defaultSchemes
values.comment:
Default URL schemes accepted by `safeHref` and `safeSrc`.

The list contains common non-script schemes.

values.name: safeHref
values.comment:
Validate a value intended for an `href` attribute.

The function returns a pair. The first value says whether the URL was accepted.
The second value contains a validated URL value or a safe fallback.

    safeHref "https://example.com"
    safeHref "javascript:alert(1)"

values.name: safeHrefW
values.comment:
Validate a value intended for an `href` attribute using whitelist schemes.

The allowed schemes are taken from the provided whitelist state.

values.name: safeSrc
values.comment:
Validate a value intended for a `src` attribute.

The function returns a pair. The first value says whether the URL was accepted.
The second value contains a validated URL value or a safe fallback.

    safeSrc "https://example.com/image.png"
    safeSrc "javascript:alert(1)"

values.name: safeSrcW
values.comment:
Validate a value intended for a `src` attribute using whitelist schemes.

The allowed schemes are taken from the provided whitelist state.

name: DetoXSS.Core

unions.name: Raw
unions.comment:
Phantom marker for raw content.

Raw content should be treated as untrusted until it is validated, sanitized, or
otherwise processed according to its output context.

unions.name: SafeContent
unions.comment:
A string wrapped with a phantom state.

The `state` parameter is used to distinguish raw, validated, and sanitized
values at the type level.

unions.name: Sanitized
unions.comment:
Phantom marker for sanitized content.

Values marked with this state are intended to represent content that has already
gone through a sanitization step.

unions.name: Validated
unions.comment:
Phantom marker for validated content.

Values marked with this state are intended to represent content that has passed
a validation step.

aliases.name: RawInput
aliases.comment:
Raw input value.

This alias is used for values that have not yet been validated or sanitized.

aliases.name: SafeHtml
aliases.comment:
Sanitized HTML-like content.

This alias is used for values that are intended to be safe to pass through the
DetoXSS API after sanitization.

aliases.name: ValidatedInput
aliases.comment:
Validated input value.

This alias is used for values that passed validation rules, for example form
input validation.

aliases.name: WhitelistState
aliases.comment: Alias for the whitelist runtime state.

values.name: defaultWhitelist
values.comment: Default whitelist configuration.

values.name: disableWhitelist
values.comment: Disable whitelist mode.

values.name: enableWhitelist
values.comment: Enable whitelist mode.

values.name: fromRaw
values.comment:
Wrap a string as raw input.

This function does not validate or sanitize the value. It only marks the value
as raw at the type level.

values.name: fromSanitized
values.comment:
Wrap a string as sanitized content.

This function does not sanitize the value by itself. It should be used only
after the caller has already sanitized the value.

For actual sanitization, use functions from `DetoXSS.Sanitize`.

values.name: fromValidated
values.comment:
Wrap a string as validated input.

This function does not perform validation by itself. It should be used only
after the caller has already validated the value.

values.name: getContent
values.comment: Extract the underlying string from a wrapped value.

values.name: initWhitelist
values.comment:
Initial whitelist state.

The default whitelist configuration is loaded, but whitelist mode is disabled.

values.name: isAttrAllowed
values.comment: Check whether an attribute is allowed by the whitelist state.

values.name: isTagAllowed
values.comment: Check whether a tag is allowed by the whitelist state.

values.name: isWhitelistEnabled
values.comment: Check whether whitelist mode is enabled.

values.name: setWhitelist
values.comment: Replace the whitelist configuration.

name: DetoXSS.ExprParser
comment:
Tolerant parser for HTML-like input.

This module converts an input string into a lightweight tree representation used
by the AST analyzer. It is intentionally tolerant because security-related input
can be malformed, incomplete, or intentionally obfuscated.

Most users should use the higher-level functions in `DetoXSS.Ast` instead of
calling this parser directly.

@docs parseExpression

@docs Parsed, HtmlNode, HtmlAttribute, Position

unions.name: HtmlNode
unions.comment:
Lightweight HTML-like node representation.

The parser distinguishes element nodes, text nodes, and comments. Element nodes
contain a tag name, attributes, child nodes, and source position.
cases: { tag : String.String, attributes : List.List DetoXSS.ExprParser.HtmlAttribute, children : List.List DetoXSS.ExprParser.HtmlNode, position : DetoXSS.ExprParser.Position }

unions.name: Parsed
unions.comment:
Result of parsing an HTML-like input.

`Ok` contains parsed HTML-like nodes. `Err` contains a parser error message.

This type is intentionally small because the parser is used as an internal
analysis step for XSS detection.

aliases.name: HtmlAttribute
aliases.comment:
HTML-like attribute representation.

Each attribute contains its normalized name, parsed value, and source position.
Boolean attributes are represented with an empty string as their value.
aliases.type: { name : String.String, value : String.String, position : DetoXSS.ExprParser.Position }

aliases.name: Position
aliases.comment:
Source position in the parsed input.

The position stores row and column numbers reported by the Elm parser.

values.name: parseExpression
values.comment:
Parse an HTML-like string into a list of lightweight nodes. The parser is tolerant and is intended for security analysis rather than browser-exact HTML parsing. parseExpression "

Hello

"

name: DetoXSS.Port

unions.name: AnalysisPolicy
unions.comment:
Policy used by analyzed port decoders.

`AllowAfterSanitize` always allows the value after sanitization.
`RejectDangerous` rejects values classified as `Dangerous`.
`RejectSuspiciousAndDangerous` rejects values classified as `Suspicious` or
`Dangerous`.

unions.name: Inbound
unions.comment:
Values that can be received through the generic inbound port decoder.

The constructors distinguish sanitized HTML-like values, validated attribute
values, URL validation results, and lists of sanitized HTML-like values.

aliases.name: HtmlResult
aliases.comment:
Result of analyzing and sanitizing an HTML-like value.

The record contains whether the value was accepted, the AST risk level, the
sanitized value, and a human-readable reason.
aliases.type: { ok : Basics.Bool, risk : DetoXSS.Ast.Risk, value : DetoXSS.Core.SafeHtml, reason : String.String }

aliases.name: UrlResult
aliases.comment:
Result of validating a URL-like value from a port.

`ok` says whether the value was accepted. `value` contains either the accepted
URL or a safe fallback.

values.name: analyzeRawHtml
values.comment:
Analyze and sanitize a raw HTML-like string.

This helper is useful when an application already has a raw string and wants to
apply the same AST policy and whitelist sanitization logic used by the analyzed
port decoders.

values.name: encodeHtmlResult
values.comment:
Encode an `HtmlResult` as JSON.

This is useful for sending analysis results back to JavaScript through an
outbound port.

values.name: encodeRisk
values.comment:
Encode an AST risk value as a JSON string.

The output is one of `"safe"`, `"suspicious"`, or `"dangerous"`.

values.name: encodeSafeHtml
values.comment: Encode sanitized HTML-like content for an outbound port.

values.name: encodeSafeHtmlList
values.comment: Encode a list of sanitized HTML-like values for an outbound port.

values.name: encodeUrlResult
values.comment: Encode a URL validation result for an outbound port.

values.name: encodeValidated
values.comment: Encode validated input for an outbound port.

values.name: encodeValidatedList
values.comment: Encode a list of validated input values for an outbound port.

values.name: inboundDecoder
values.comment:
Decode an inbound JSON value from a port.

The decoder expects an object with a `type` field and a `value` field. The
`type` field decides whether the value is treated as HTML-like content,
attribute content, URL content, or a list of HTML-like values.

values.name: inboundDecoderAnalyzed
values.comment:
Decode inbound port data and apply AST analysis for HTML-like values.

The provided policy controls whether suspicious or dangerous values are accepted
or rejected. Non-HTML values are decoded using the same rules as
`inboundDecoder`.

values.name: maxChars
values.comment: Maximum number of characters accepted by capped port decoders.

values.name: maxListLen
values.comment: Maximum number of items accepted by list decoders.

values.name: normalizeNewlines
values.comment:
Normalize newline characters.

This converts Windows and old Mac line endings to `\n`.

values.name: rawDecoderCapped
values.comment:
Decode a raw string with length limiting.

The decoder fails if the value exceeds `maxChars`.

values.name: safeHtmlDecoderAnalyzedW
values.comment:
Decode a single HTML-like string with whitelist sanitization and AST policy.

If the policy rejects the detected risk level, decoding fails.

values.name: safeHtmlDecoderW
values.comment: Decode an HTML-like string and sanitize it with a whitelist state.

values.name: safeHtmlListDecoderAnalyzedW
values.comment:
Decode a list of HTML-like strings with whitelist sanitization and AST policy.

If any item is rejected by the policy, the whole decoder fails.

values.name: safeHtmlListDecoderW
values.comment:
Decode a list of HTML-like strings and sanitize every item.

The decoder fails if the list exceeds `maxListLen`.

values.name: safeHtmlResultDecoderW
values.comment:
Decode a single HTML-like string and return an analysis result.

Unlike `safeHtmlDecoderAnalyzedW`, this decoder returns a structured
`HtmlResult` containing the risk and rejection reason instead of only returning
the sanitized value.

values.name: stripControl
values.comment:
Remove control characters from a string.

Newline, carriage return, and tab characters are preserved.

values.name: urlDecoderW
values.comment: Decode a URL-like string and validate it with whitelist URL schemes.

values.name: validatedAttrDecoder
values.comment: Decode an attribute-like string and wrap it as validated input.

name: DetoXSS.Sanitize

values.name: decodeBasicEntities
values.comment:
Decode a small set of common HTML entities.

This helper is used before sanitization so that already encoded input can be
normalized before being escaped again.

values.name: encodeHtml
values.comment:
Escape HTML-sensitive characters.

This converts characters such as `<`, `>`, `&`, `"`, and `'` into HTML
entities so that the value can be displayed as text instead of being interpreted
as markup.

values.name: sanitizeForAttribute
values.comment:
Sanitize a value intended for an attribute-like context.

This removes control characters, collapses whitespace, and wraps the result as
validated input. It does not make arbitrary attribute usage safe in every
possible context.

values.name: sanitizeText
values.comment:
Sanitize input that should be displayed as plain text.

The whole input is treated as text. HTML tags are not preserved.

    sanitizeText "<script>alert(1)</script>"

values.name: sanitizeWithWhitelist
values.comment:
Sanitize HTML-like input using a whitelist state. When whitelist mode is enabled, only allowed tags and attributes are preserved. When whitelist mode is disabled, the whole input is escaped as text. sanitizeWithWhitelist whitelistState "

Hello

"

values.name: stripControl
values.comment:
Remove control characters from a string.

Newline, carriage return, and tab characters are preserved.

name: DetoXSS.Validate

unions.name: ValidationError
unions.comment:
Possible validation errors returned by the validation helpers.

These errors describe why a value did not pass a validation rule.

aliases.name: Rule
aliases.comment:
Validation rule.

A rule receives a string and returns a list of validation errors. An empty list
means that the value passed that rule.

values.name: allowedChars
values.comment:
Require the value to match a regular expression.

The description is used in the `InvalidChars` error when the value does not
match.

values.name: matchesRegex
values.comment: Create a validation rule from a regular expression and custom error.

values.name: maxLength
values.comment: Require a maximum string length.

values.name: minLength
values.comment: Require a minimum string length.

values.name: nonEmpty
values.comment: Require a non-empty value.

values.name: validate
values.comment:
Run a list of validation rules against a string.

The input is trimmed before validation. If all rules pass, the cleaned value is
returned as `ValidatedInput`.
values.type: List.List DetoXSS.Validate.Rule -> String.String -> Result.Result (List.List DetoXSS.Validate.ValidationError) DetoXSS.Core.ValidatedInput

values.name: validateEmail
values.comment:
Validate a simple email address.

This checks the general shape of an email address. It does not verify whether
the address actually exists.

values.name: validatePassword
values.comment:
Validate a password.

The password must be non-empty, have a minimum length, and contain a mixture of
character classes.

values.name: validatePhone
values.comment:
Validate a phone-like value.

The value may contain digits, spaces, plus signs, dashes, and parentheses.

values.name: validateUsername
values.comment:
Validate a username.

The username must be non-empty, have an allowed length, and contain only
letters, digits, underscores, dots, or dashes.

name: DetoXSS.Whitelist

aliases.name: State
aliases.comment:
Runtime state of whitelist sanitization.

The `active` field controls whether whitelist mode is enabled. When whitelist
mode is disabled, sanitization treats the whole input as text instead of
preserving HTML tags.

aliases.name: Whitelist
aliases.comment:
Whitelist configuration used by `sanitizeWithWhitelist`.

The configuration contains four lists:

  - `tags` are HTML tag names that may be preserved.
  - `attributes` are attribute names that may be preserved.
  - `urlAttributes` are attributes whose values should be treated as URLs.
  - `allowedSchemes` are URL schemes accepted in URL-like attributes.

The configuration is normalized when it is created through helper functions such
as `fromLists`, `fromAll`, or `fromAllFull`.
aliases.type: { tags : List.List String.String, attributes : List.List String.String, urlAttributes : List.List String.String, allowedSchemes : List.List String.String }

values.name: blockedAttributes
values.comment:
Attributes that are blocked regardless of whitelist configuration.

This includes style-related and document-embedding attributes. Inline event
handler attributes such as `onclick` and `onerror` are also blocked by
`isBlockedAttribute`.

values.name: blockedSchemes
values.comment: URL schemes that are blocked regardless of whitelist configuration.

values.name: blockedTags
values.comment:
Tags that are blocked regardless of whitelist configuration.

These tags are considered too risky to preserve in sanitized HTML.

values.name: defaultConfig
values.comment:
Default whitelist configuration.

It allows a small set of common formatting tags, link and image attributes, and
common URL schemes such as `http:`, `https:`, `mailto:`, and `tel:`.

values.name: disable
values.comment:
Disable whitelist-based sanitization.

When disabled, `sanitizeWithWhitelist` treats the whole input as text.

values.name: enable
values.comment:
Enable whitelist-based sanitization.

When enabled, `sanitizeWithWhitelist` may preserve allowed tags and attributes.

values.name: fromAll
values.comment:
Create a whitelist from allowed tags, attributes, and URL schemes.

The URL-like attributes default to `href` and `src`.

    fromAll
        [ "p", "a" ]
        [ "href" ]
        [ "http:", "https:" ]
values.type: List.List String.String -> List.List String.String -> List.List String.String -> DetoXSS.Whitelist.Whitelist

values.name: fromAllFull
values.comment:
Create a full whitelist configuration.

This variant allows configuring allowed tags, allowed attributes, URL-like
attributes, and allowed URL schemes explicitly.

    fromAllFull
        [ "p", "a", "img" ]
        [ "href", "src", "alt" ]
        [ "href", "src" ]
        [ "http:", "https:" ]
values.type: List.List String.String -> List.List String.String -> List.List String.String -> List.List String.String -> DetoXSS.Whitelist.Whitelist

values.name: fromLists
values.comment:
Create a whitelist from allowed tags and attributes.

The URL-like attributes default to `href` and `src`. The allowed URL schemes
default to `defaultConfig.allowedSchemes`.

    fromLists [ "p", "strong", "a" ] [ "href", "title" ]

values.name: get
values.comment: Get allowed tags and attributes from the current state.
values.type: DetoXSS.Whitelist.State -> { tags : List.List String.String, attributes : List.List String.String }

values.name: getAll
values.comment: Get allowed tags, attributes, and URL schemes from the current state.
values.type: DetoXSS.Whitelist.State -> { tags : List.List String.String, attributes : List.List String.String, schemes : List.List String.String }

values.name: getAllFull
values.comment:
Get the full whitelist configuration from the current state.

This includes allowed tags, allowed attributes, URL-like attributes, and allowed
URL schemes.
values.type: DetoXSS.Whitelist.State -> { tags : List.List String.String, attributes : List.List String.String, urlAttributes : List.List String.String, schemes : List.List String.String }

values.name: getAllowedSchemes
values.comment:
Get allowed URL schemes.

If whitelist mode is disabled, the default scheme list is returned.

values.name: initialState
values.comment:
Initial whitelist state.

The default configuration is loaded, but whitelist mode is disabled.

Use `enable` to activate whitelist-based sanitization.

values.name: isAllowedAttribute
values.comment: Check whether an attribute is allowed by the current whitelist configuration.

values.name: isAllowedTag
values.comment: Check whether a tag is allowed by the current whitelist configuration.

values.name: isBlockedAttribute
values.comment:
Check whether an attribute is hard-blocked.

This returns `True` for inline event handlers such as `onclick`, for attributes
listed in `blockedAttributes`, and for namespace declarations starting with
`xmlns`.

values.name: isBlockedScheme
values.comment: Check whether a URL scheme is hard-blocked.

values.name: isBlockedTag
values.comment: Check whether a tag is hard-blocked.

values.name: isEnabled
values.comment: Check whether whitelist mode is enabled.

values.name: isUrlAttribute
values.comment:
Check whether an attribute should be treated as URL-like.

URL-like attributes are validated against the allowed URL schemes during
sanitization.

values.name: set
values.comment:
Replace the current whitelist configuration.

The configuration is normalized before being stored. Blocked tags, blocked
attributes, and blocked schemes are removed during normalization.

values.name: setAttributes
values.comment: Replace the allowed attributes in the current whitelist state.

values.name: setSchemes
values.comment:
Replace the allowed URL schemes in the current whitelist state.

Schemes are normalized to lowercase and always end with `:`.

values.name: setUrlAttributes
values.comment:
Replace the URL-like attributes in the current whitelist state.

URL-like attributes are checked against allowed URL schemes during
sanitization.
